items.name: Multimodal-Machine-Learning-and-Data-Analysis-Framework-for-Depression-Detection
items.description: Master's thesis codebase for evaluation of fusion strategies in clinical depression screening. Multimodal depression detection framework using Text, Audio, and Video modalities with Early and Late Fusion strategies. Implements Logistic Regression and XGBoost classifiers on E-DAIC dataset with SHAP and coefficient-based methods.

items.description: This project presents a physics-inspired spacecraft telemetry simulation and anomaly detection framework that models power, thermal, and communication subsystems in Low Earth Orbit. It combines engineering-based thresholds and machine learning to analyze synthetic telemetry and study fault detection and spacecraft health monitoring.

items.description: A multi-level obfuscation framework (mild, moderate, strong) for privacy-preserving machine learning on network-traffic data. Includes dataset balancing, obfuscation scripts, TabNet models, baseline ML benchmarks, and reproducible experiments.

items.name: mlinternship
items.description: End-to-end ML pipeline for analyzing and mitigating bias in machine learning models. Features bias detection (SPD, EOD, DIR metrics), multiple classifiers (Random Forest, Logistic Regression, SVM), and a configurable YAML-driven training framework.

items.name: Stock-Prediction-Project
items.description: A Python-based machine learning framework that predicts Tesla's daily stock returns by combining FinBERT transformer sentiment analysis of Elon Musk's tweets with traditional OHLCV technical indicators. The sentiment-enhanced model achieved a statistically significant R² improvement of 0.5% over the baseline, validated through bootstrap resampling.

It leverages sequence-to-sequence architectures, attention mechanisms, and transformer-based models to deliver accurate, context-aware, and scalable translation across multiple languages in real time.

items.name: Real-Time-Anomaly-Detection-in-loT-Network
items.description: Real-Time Anomaly Detection in IoT Networks is a streaming analytics framework that monitors IoT device data to detect anomalies, failures, and security threats in real time. It leverages statistical methods and machine learning to identify unusual patterns, trigger alerts, and ensure system reliability, safety, and operational continuity.

items.name: Evaluation-as-a-Service-EaaS-
items.description: Evaluation-as-a-Service (EaaS) is a scalable, production-ready evaluation platform designed to standardize, automate, and operationalize model assessment across machine learning and AI systems. It provides a centralized framework for benchmarking, monitoring, and validating model performance through reproducible, API-driven evaluation workflows.

items.name: Drift-Aware-Retraining-Pipeline
items.description: Drift Aware Retraining Pipeline is a production-ready machine learning infrastructure framework designed to detect data and concept drift and automatically trigger intelligent model retraining workflows. The system ensures sustained model performance in dynamic, real-world environments where data distributions continuously evolve.
